CLF-C02,"Domain 4: Billing & Pricing","Billing, Pricing, and Support","## CLF-C02 Domain 4: Billing, Pricing, and Support
**Weight:** ~12% of scored exam content (smallest domain)
**Exam Level:** Foundational
This domain tests your understanding of how AWS charges you, how to manage and optimize costs, and what support options are available.
## AWS Pricing Fundamentals
### Core Pricing Principles

column3: Pay only for what you consume; no upfront costs
column4: EC2 billed per second; S3 per GB
Savings Plans, Reserved Instances (up to 72% off)
S3 storage tiers; data transfer tiers

column1: ### What's Always Free

column3: Data INTO AWS is always free
No charge for users, groups, roles, policies
No charge for the service itself
No charge (you pay for resources created)
No charge (you pay for underlying resources)

column1: ### What Costs Money (Common Charges)

column4: Use VPC endpoints for AWS services

column1: ## Compute Pricing Models
### EC2 Pricing Options

column3: Commit to instance family in region
Commit to specific instance for 1-3 years
Same but can change instance type
Bid on unused capacity; 2-min interruption notice

column1: ### Savings Plans vs. Reserved Instances

column3: Can change instance type, OS, region

column1: ### Lambda Pricing

column3: Zero cost when not running (unlike EC2)

column1: ### S3 Pricing

column3: Per GB per month (varies by class)
Per GB leaving S3 to internet

column1: ## AWS Free Tier
### Three Types of Free Tier Offers

column4: Lambda (1M requests/month); DynamoDB (25 GB); CloudWatch (10 metrics)
EC2 (750 hrs/month t2.micro); S3 (5 GB); RDS (750 hrs/month)

column1: ### Key Free Tier Limits (Most Tested)
### Free Tier Alerts
- AWS Budgets can alert when Free Tier usage approaches limits.
- Automatic email at 85% of Free Tier limit.
- Billing Dashboard shows current Free Tier usage.
## Cost Management Tools
### AWS Cost Explorer - Key Features

column3: Suggests optimal plans based on usage

column1: ### AWS Budgets - Types
**Budget Actions:** Send email/SNS notification, apply IAM policy to restrict launches, stop instances when exceeded.
## Consolidated Billing and AWS Organizations
### Consolidated Billing Benefits

column3: Combined usage qualifies for tier pricing
Reserved capacity shared across all accounts

column1: ### How Volume Discounts Work
Account A: 40 TB S3 storage
Account B: 35 TB S3 storage
Account C: 25 TB S3 storage
Combined: 100 TB -> qualifies for higher volume discount tier
Without consolidation: Each pays individual (higher) rate
With consolidation: All benefit from combined volume pricing
### Cost Allocation Tags
- Filter costs in Cost Explorer by tag.
- Create budgets per tag (per project or team).
- **Must be activated in Billing Console** before appearing in reports.
## AWS Support Plans
### Key Support Concepts

column3: Alerts about events affecting YOUR resources (free)

column1: ## Cost Optimization Strategies

column3: Use smallest instance that meets requirements
Remove unattached EBS, unused EIPs, idle instances

## Data Transfer Pricing Summary
## Key Exam Patterns for CLF-C02 Domain 4
**Pricing:**
- **""Pay only for what you use""** -> On-Demand pricing
- **""Lowest cost for steady-state""** -> Savings Plans or Reserved Instances
- **""Lowest cost for interruptible workloads""** -> Spot Instances (up to 90%)
- **""No charge for data transfer""** -> Inbound (always free) or same-AZ private IP
- **""Charged even when instance stopped""** -> EBS volumes (provisioned storage)
- **""NOT charged""** -> IAM, VPC, Auto Scaling, CloudFormation, Beanstalk
**Cost Management:**
- **""Analyze past spending""** -> Cost Explorer
- **""Set spending alerts""** -> AWS Budgets
- **""Estimate costs before building""** -> Pricing Calculator
- **""Most detailed billing data""** -> Cost and Usage Report (CUR)
- **""Detect unusual spending""** -> Cost Anomaly Detection
- **""Right-sizing recommendations""** -> Compute Optimizer / Cost Explorer
- **""Track costs by team""** -> Cost Allocation Tags
- **""Share RI savings across accounts""** -> Consolidated Billing
**Support:**
- **""24/7 phone support""** -> Business or higher
- **""Designated TAM""** -> Enterprise only
- **""All Trusted Advisor checks""** -> Business or higher
- **""< 15 minute response""** -> Enterprise
- **""Free support resources""** -> Documentation, re:Post, Health Dashboard
- **""Billing questions""** -> Concierge (Enterprise)
**Free Tier:**
- **""750 hours/month EC2""** -> 12 months free (t2.micro)
- **""1 million Lambda requests""** -> Always free
- **""Avoid unexpected charges""** -> Budgets + Free Tier alerts
## Quick Exam Tips for Domain 4
- **Data transfer IN is always free.** Data OUT costs money. Tested frequently.
- **Savings Plans** are preferred over Reserved Instances (more flexible; apply to Lambda/Fargate too).
- **Spot Instances = up to 90% off** BUT can be interrupted. Only for fault-tolerant workloads.
- **Consolidated Billing** shares volume discounts AND RI/SP benefits across all accounts.
- **Cost Explorer** = analyze past. **Budgets** = alert on future. **Pricing Calculator** = estimate before building.
- **Trusted Advisor full checks** require Business support or higher.
- **TAM** = Enterprise only. Enterprise On-Ramp = pool of TAMs (not designated).
- **EBS volumes charged even when EC2 is stopped.** Common gotcha question.
- **Unused Elastic IPs cost money.** Charged when NOT attached to running instance.
- **Cost Allocation Tags must be activated** in Billing Console to appear in reports.
- **CloudFront reduces costs** - cheaper egress rates than direct EC2/S3.
- **Auto Scaling, CloudFormation, Beanstalk** are free services - you pay for resources they create.
## Billing Tools Quick Reference
WHAT DO YOU NEED?                          -> USE THIS
See where money is going (past)            -> Cost Explorer
Get alerts before overspending             -> AWS Budgets
Estimate costs before building             -> AWS Pricing Calculator
Most detailed billing data                 -> Cost and Usage Report (CUR)
Detect unusual spending                    -> Cost Anomaly Detection
Right-sizing recommendations               -> Compute Optimizer
Track costs by team/project                -> Cost Allocation Tags
Share discounts across accounts            -> Consolidated Billing
Find wasted resources                      -> Trusted Advisor (Business+)
Check Free Tier usage                      -> Billing Dashboard / Budgets
Buy third-party software on AWS bill       -> AWS Marketplace
## Summary
This covers all objectives in CLF-C02 Domain 4:
- **Pricing fundamentals** - Pay-as-you-go, volume discounts, no termination fees
- **Compute pricing** - On-Demand, Savings Plans, Reserved, Spot, Dedicated
- **Free Tier** - Always Free, 12 Months Free, Trials; key limits
- **Cost management tools** - Cost Explorer, Budgets, Pricing Calculator, CUR
- **Consolidated Billing** - Organizations, volume discounts, shared reservations, tags
- **Support plans** - Basic through Enterprise; TAM, Concierge, response times
- **Cost optimization** - Right-sizing, serverless, lifecycle policies, delete waste
- **Billing management** - Dashboard, Marketplace, Service Quotas"
